reasoning: A dominant global eastward atmospheric circulation at mid-to-upper troposphere is the jet stream — a direct consequence of the Coriolis effect on a rotating sphere with a meridional temperature gradient. This has been observed since WWII-era high-altitude bombing missions and is fundamental to meteorology. However, the claim of a SINGLE global eastward rotation oversimplifies dramatically: the atmosphere has multiple cells (Hadley, Ferrel, Polar) with different flow directions at different latitudes, the tropical easterlies (trade winds) blow westward, and the stratospheric quasi-biennial oscillation reverses direction every ~28 months. A "single global eastward rotation" is contradicted by the observed multi-cell structure. To the extent the claim is correct (mid-latitude westerlies dominate), it is standard physics; to the extent it claims a SINGLE rotation, it is contradicted by trade winds and polar easterlies.
